street.thesis: Analog/embedded inventory correction has ended; FCF per share re-rates sharply as capex normalizes from $5B peak to $2-3B; industrial up 30%+ YoY and data center up 90% YoY in Q1-2026 are the two new growth drivers; 300mm manufacturing moat translates to structural gross margin expansion toward 65%+. Silicon Labs adds embedded wireless connectivity. The call on the stock is a FCF-per-share inflection, priced at 36x NTM EPS.

history_context.note: FCF/share has been extremely volatile: $5.74/share at peak (FY2022 on $5.9B FCF / ~1.03B shares), then collapsed to ~$1.55/share (FY2024: $1.50B FCF / 0.92B shares) as the capex super-cycle depressed FCF. The capex cycle is the dominant FCF driver, not revenue. A pure 5-year CAGR on FCF is meaningless here; the correct anchor is FCF at normalization (FY2026-2027 consensus $6.92-$8.00+/share).

duality_decomposition.note: single-stage justified forward P/E = (1 - g/ROIC)/(r - g); shows the ROIC/payout lever

peers.name: Analog Devices
peers.note: ADI trades at ~34x NTM FCF; similar analog franchise but no 300mm moat and higher leverage post-Maxim. Gross margin ~68% but structurally fabless at 200mm.

peers.name: Microchip Technology
peers.note: MCHP at ~22x NTM FCF; similar Embedded/MCU exposure but significantly higher leverage (~8x net debt/EBITDA post-Microsemi) and lower analog quality mix. Discount vs TXN is warranted.

peers.name: STMicroelectronics
peers.note: STM at ~15x NTM FCF; more automotive-MCU-power discrete mix; European listing; lower multi-year FCF growth visibility. Significant discount to TXN reflects higher cyclicality and absence of 300mm moat.

peers.name: NXP Semiconductors
peers.note: NXP at ~18x NTM FCF; highly automotive-concentrated (~70%), recovering in 2026 alongside TXN. Less analog content exposure, more MCU-dominated.

peers.name: onsemi
peers.note: onsemi (ON) at ~20x NTM FCF; SiC and power management focus, heavy EV exposure. Comparable auto exposure but different technology (SiC vs TXN's analog). Peer FCF yield and multiple midpoint.

reconstruction.what_price_implies: At 42.5x NTM FCF/share (consensus CY2026), the current price implies a ~12.1% FCF CAGR for 10y (then 4% terminal) at r=9.0%.
reconstruction.what_street_implies: The street mean target implies ~10.9% for 10y.
reconstruction.history_anchor: FCF/share has been extremely volatile: $5.74/share at peak (FY2022 on $5.9B FCF / ~1.03B shares), then collapsed to ~$1.55/share (FY2024: $1.50B FCF / 0.92B shares) as the capex super-cycle depressed FCF. The capex cycle is the dominant FCF driver, not revenue. A pure 5-year CAGR on FCF is meaningless here; the correct anchor is FCF at normalization (FY2026-2027 consensus $6.92-$8.00+/share).
reconstruction.contested_lever: FCF per share at normalized capex ($6.92 consensus, >$8 management target, $8.20 Theia base) AND the sustainable exit FCF multiple the market applies once capex normalizes. The call hinges on: (1) does industrial recovery continue at current pace or stall? (2) is the 300mm manufacturing moat durable enough to justify a premium multiple above peer group? At 47x NTM FCF vs the peer group at 15-34x, TXN already prices a structural premium — the crux is whether that premium is earned.
